answer:
Mailchimp supports two primary authentication methods: API keys and OAuth 2.0 tokens [1]. Use an API key for applications tightly coupled to a single account [1][2], and use OAuth 2.0 when accessing data on behalf of other Mailchimp users [2][3].

Requests can be authenticated via HTTP Basic Authentication (using your API key) or Bearer Authentication (using an OAuth token or API key) [1].

For Basic Authentication, provide your key as the password [1]:
```bash
curl --request GET \
--url 'https://<dc>.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/' \
--user 'anystring:YOUR_API_KEY'
```

For Bearer Authentication, pass the token in the header [1]:
```bash
curl --request GET \
--url 'https://<dc>.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/' \
--header "Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>"
```

API access is tied to the authorizing user's role and account permissions [1][4].
